text: Border studies examines the process of the formation of borders, their representation, destabilisation, transposition, invasion or destruction. The Border Studies field of knowledge has developed strongly since the 1980s in a wide variety of fields and scientific areas in different parts of the world. It has become central to the debate concerning the consequences of globalisation following the collapse of the Cold War. At the level of everyday life it is known that the borders or borders of the states and the European Union do not anymore represent a rigid barrier in the respect of physical exchanges of people, commodities and financial capital and less still in the respect of less material exchange of news, values and ideas. However, this relative weakness of the borders on the one hand enables new cooperative or economic and cultural space to develop; on the other hand, the open nature of borders which admit diverse current of people, goods and values creates fear of the invasion of foreign cultures. It is from this contradictory aspect of the process of globalisation which has given a powerful impulse to border studies, the phenomenon which had its beginning in the USA.
